title: miguelia annamica
intro: Miguelia annamica, a plant native to Southeast Asia, belongs to the Rutaceae family and is commonly known as Vietnamese rue or Vietnamese bitter orange. Traditionally, Miguelia annamica has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as aiding digestion, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Miguelia annamica is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a variety of health conditions.
